loot_tables.note: Loot is generated by combining currency rolls, magic item probability checks, and valuables rolls. All dice expressions use standard notation (e.g. '2d6*10' means roll 2d6 and multiply by 10). Source types: 'enemy_normal', 'exploration', 'treasure_room', 'boss'.

tier_definitions.note: Player level determines the tier, which controls all loot scaling.

tiers.name: Tier 1 — Adventurer
tiers.description: Fledgling heroes. Wealth is scarce, magic is rare.

tiers.name: Tier 2 — Hero
tiers.description: Proven adventurers. Gold flows more freely, uncommon magic appears regularly.

tiers.name: Tier 3 — Champion
tiers.description: Legendary figures. Rare magic is expected, fortunes change hands.

tiers.name: Tier 4 — Paragon
tiers.description: World-shaping power. Very rare and legendary items are in reach.

enemy_normal.name: Enemy Normal
enemy_normal.description: Loot taken from a defeated enemy in standard combat. Reflects what the creature was carrying.
enemy_normal.flavor: What does this creature actually own? Weapons, a few coins, maybe a single useful item — nothing more.

exploration.name: Exploration
exploration.description: Items found while exploring — hidden caches, abandoned rooms, forgotten alcoves, bodies that were never looted.
exploration.flavor: Something left behind. Could be anything, could be nothing.

treasure_room.name: Treasure Room
treasure_room.description: A dedicated vault, hoard, or cache. Someone put these things here deliberately.
treasure_room.flavor: This was meant to be valuable. Coins, objects, sometimes magic — all gathered in one place.

boss.name: Boss
boss.description: Loot from a unique enemy, a powerful creature, or the culmination of a dungeon. Thematic and significant.
boss.flavor: This creature was someone. What they carried reflects who they were.

currency_tables.note: Roll the specified dice and apply the multiplier. Results are in the listed denomination. 'chance' is a 0-100 percentage — roll d100, on a result equal to or below the chance value that currency type is present.

magic_item_probability.note: Roll d100. If result <= chance value, a magic item is present. If guaranteed (chance=100), always generate one.

rarity_weights.note: When a magic item is found, roll d100 to determine rarity. Weights are cumulative thresholds.

item_type_pools.note: Each source type has a weighted pool of item categories. Roll d100 to determine category, then select a random item of the determined rarity from that category in your item database.

boss.note: Unique item tied to boss identity

boss_thematic_tags.note: When generating a boss item, first check if the boss has a creature type tag. If so, use this table to bias the item category and suggest a thematic keyword for querying the item database.

undead.flavor: Items that channel or command the dead, or that leach life.

fiend.flavor: Items forged in the Lower Planes or tied to diabolical power.

dragon.flavor: Items made from or empowered by draconic material.

fey.flavor: Items with an otherworldly elegance — beautiful and unreliable.

humanoid_warrior.flavor: Well-maintained military equipment, possibly bearing a house insignia.

humanoid_mage.flavor: Tools of the arcane trade — a mage travels with their instruments.

beast.flavor: Natural materials of great potency — venom, hide, bone, gland.

construct.flavor: Components of the construct itself that retain magical properties.

celestial.flavor: Items that radiate goodness — warm to the touch, softly luminous.

aberration.flavor: Items that feel wrong — alien geometry, unsettling properties.

valuables_tables.note: Valuables are non-magical objects of significant worth: gems, art objects, jewelry, rare materials. Roll d100 to determine if valuables are present (see chance per tier/source), then roll on the appropriate table to determine what was found. Valuables can be sold for their listed gp value.

tier_1.name: Banded Agate
tier_1.description: Translucent striped brown, blue, white, or red.

tier_1.name: Eye Agate
tier_1.description: Translucent circles of gray, white, brown, blue, or green.

tier_1.name: Lapis Lazuli
tier_1.description: Opaque light and dark blue with yellow flecks.

tier_2.name: Bloodstone
tier_2.description: Opaque dark gray with red flecks.

tier_2.name: Moonstone
tier_2.description: Translucent white with pale blue hue.

tier_2.name: Onyx
tier_2.description: Opaque bands of black and white.

tier_3.name: Amber
tier_3.description: Transparent watery gold to rich gold.

tier_3.name: Jade
tier_3.description: Translucent white or green, spotted with deeper green.

tier_3.name: Spinel
tier_3.description: Transparent red, red-brown, or deep green.

tier_4.name: Diamond
tier_4.description: Transparent blue-white, canary, pink, brown, or blue.

tier_4.name: Ruby
tier_4.description: Transparent clear red to deep crimson.

tier_4.name: Star Ruby
tier_4.description: Translucent ruby with white star-shaped center.

tier_1.name: Silver ewer
tier_1.description: A decorative silver pitcher with floral engravings.

tier_1.name: Carved bone statuette
tier_1.description: A small figure carved from animal bone, worn smooth.

tier_1.name: Small gold bracelet
tier_1.description: A simple band of gold, unadorned.

tier_1.name: Cloth-of-gold vestment
tier_1.description: A piece of ceremonial clothing woven with gold thread.

tier_1.name: Black velvet mask
tier_1.description: A fine mask stitched with silver thread.

tier_1.name: Copper chalice
tier_1.description: An ornate drinking cup with verdigris details.

tier_1.name: Painted portrait
tier_1.description: A small oil portrait of a person of evident importance.

tier_2.name: Gold ring with bloodstone
tier_2.description: A heavy gold band set with a polished bloodstone.

tier_2.name: Silver statuette
tier_2.description: A detailed figure of an animal or deity, palm-sized.

tier_2.name: Ivory drinking horn
tier_2.description: A carved and decorated drinking horn banded with silver.

tier_2.name: Brass music box
tier_2.description: A mechanical box that plays a brief, haunting melody.

tier_2.name: Silk embroidered gloves
tier_2.description: Exquisitely embroidered formal gloves in a noble style.

tier_2.name: Small mirror in gold frame
tier_2.description: A hand mirror with a gilded frame carved with vines.

tier_2.name: Obsidian figurine
tier_2.description: A polished black figurine, likely religious in purpose.

tier_2.name: Tapestry fragment
tier_2.description: A section of an ornate tapestry depicting a battle or myth.

tier_3.name: Gold idol
tier_3.description: A solid gold figure of a deity or significant figure.

tier_3.name: Platinum bracelet
tier_3.description: A broad platinum band engraved with geometric patterns.

tier_3.name: Painting by a master
tier_3.description: A canvas painting of undeniable quality and age.

tier_3.name: Silver and gold crown
tier_3.description: A ceremonial crown — too ornate for battle, too real for theatre.

tier_3.name: Carved ivory box
tier_3.description: A box carved from a single piece of ivory with intricate scenes.

tier_3.name: Ancient reliquary
tier_3.description: A decorated container meant to hold sacred remains. Empty.

tier_3.name: Electrum necklace
tier_3.description: A long chain of electrum links with a teardrop pendant.

tier_3.name: Jade dragon figurine
tier_3.description: A large carved jade piece, possibly a ceremonial object.

tier_4.name: Diamond-studded throne
tier_4.description: A portable ceremonial throne inlaid with cut diamonds.

tier_4.name: Ruby-set crown
tier_4.description: A genuine royal crown set with rubies the size of thumbs.

tier_4.name: Platinum chalice
tier_4.description: A massive ceremonial cup of solid platinum, almost too heavy to hold.

tier_4.name: Ancient map on vellum
tier_4.description: A detailed map of a place or region that no longer exists — or should not.

tier_4.name: Set of twelve sapphire seals
tier_4.description: Twelve wax-seal stamps in gold mounts, each set with a sapphire.

tier_4.name: Emerald-inlaid armor panel
tier_4.description: A single ornate breastplate panel from a suit never completed.

tier_4.name: Colossal pearl
tier_4.description: A single pearl the size of a fist, deep black with an inner light.

tier_4.name: Illuminated manuscript
tier_4.description: A hand-illuminated tome of historical or religious significance.

quantity_rolls.note: For treasure rooms and boss hoards, roll this many magic items and valuables before applying probability checks.

generation_algorithm.note: Step-by-step procedure for the generateLoot(playerLevel, source) function.

steps.name: Determine Tier
steps.logic: If level 1-4 → tier_1. If 5-10 → tier_2. If 11-16 → tier_3. If 17-20 → tier_4.

steps.name: Roll Currency
steps.logic: For each entry in currency_tables[tier][source]: roll d100, if result <= chance, roll the dice expression and add the result in that denomination to the output.

steps.name: Check for Magic Items
steps.logic: Roll d100. If result <= magic_item_probability[tier][source].chance, proceed to step 4. Otherwise skip to step 7. For treasure_room and boss at high tiers, also check minimum_rarity.

steps.name: Determine Number of Magic Items
steps.logic: Roll the dice in quantity_rolls.magic_items[source] to determine how many items to generate.

steps.name: Determine Rarity
steps.logic: For each item: roll d100 and compare to rarity_weights[tier] cumulative thresholds. If minimum_rarity applies, re-roll until rarity meets or exceeds the minimum.

steps.name: Determine Item Type
steps.logic: Roll d100 against item_type_pools[source] cumulative weights to get a category. For boss source, first check if a creature type tag is available in boss_thematic_tags and apply preferred_categories bias. Then select a random item of the determined rarity and category from the item database.

steps.name: Check for Valuables
steps.logic: Roll d100. If result <= valuables_tables.valuable_presence_chance[tier][source], proceed to step 8.

steps.name: Determine Valuables
steps.logic: Roll the dice in quantity_rolls.valuables[source]. For each: roll d100 to pick from either gems[tier] or art_objects[tier] (50/50 split), then roll d100 on the chosen table and match to the appropriate roll_max entry.

steps.name: Assemble Output
steps.logic: Return: { currency: {...}, magic_items: [...], valuables: [...], total_gp_value: number }. total_gp_value = currency converted to gp + sum of valuable values + sum of magic item base values.
